global.title: ecosystem map
global.content:
# ecosystem map

The complete Powerhouse document model ecosystem as reported by switchboard CLI. 21 domain models, 346 total operations, organized into 5 functional zones.

## Service & Pricing (6 models, 155 ops)

The core of this project. These models define the product catalog, commercialization, and subscription lifecycle.

- [[facet model provides configurable classification dimensions]] — 6 ops, categorical options consumed by templates and offerings
- [[resource-template model defines reusable service catalog blueprints]] — 28 ops, product-level configuration without pricing
- [[service-offering model defines tiered pricing with option groups and service groups]] — 51 ops (largest), tiered pricing and configuration
- [[service-subscription model tracks service-level subscription agreements]] — 12 ops, agreement-level subscription tracking
- [[subscription-instance model tracks active customer subscriptions with lifecycle state]] — 39 ops, individual customer subscription with billing
- [[resource-instance model tracks provisioned resource lifecycle]] — 19 ops, provisioned resources with lifecycle state machine

Data flow: Facet → ResourceTemplate → ServiceOffering → ServiceSubscription → SubscriptionInstance → ResourceInstance

## Finance & Billing (4 models, 37 ops)

Financial recording and billing infrastructure.

- [[accounts model defines the chart of accounts structure]] — 4 ops, foundation account entities
- [[account-transactions model tracks financial transaction records]] — 10 ops, debit/credit recording
- [[billing-statement model captures periodic billing summaries]] — 7 ops, periodic billing aggregation
- [[payment-terms model defines payment conditions and schedules]] — 16 ops, payment conditions and terms

## Reporting (3 models, 67 ops)

Output documents — invoices, expense claims, snapshots.

- [[invoice model generates formal payment requests]] — 31 ops (second largest), formal invoice generation
- [[expense-report model tracks expense claims and approvals]] — 18 ops, expense claim workflow
- [[snapshot-report model provides point-in-time data captures]] — 18 ops, point-in-time archival

## Organization & Identity (4 models, 29 ops)

Who operates in the ecosystem — builders, teams, networks.

- [[builder-profile model describes individual contributor identities]] — 12 ops, individual contributor profiles (referenced by SO.operatorId)
- [[builders model aggregates builder collections]] — 2 ops (smallest), builder directory
- [[network-profile model represents network-level organizational entities]] — 11 ops, DAOs and ecosystem networks
- [[operational-hub-profile model defines operational hub configurations]] — 4 ops, teams and working groups

## Work Management (3 models, 48 ops)

Project scoping, procurement, and work organization.

- [[scope-of-work model structures deliverables and milestones]] — 33 ops (third largest), project deliverables and budgets
- [[request-for-proposals model manages procurement workflows]] — 6 ops, procurement funnel
- [[workstream model organizes ongoing work streams]] — 9 ops, portfolio-level grouping

## Cross-Zone Dependencies

- ServiceOffering.`operatorId` → BuilderProfile (Service & Pricing → Organization)
- SubscriptionInstance → BillingStatement (Service & Pricing → Finance)
- SubscriptionInstance → Invoice (Service & Pricing → Reporting)
- ScopeOfWork → Invoice (Work Management → Reporting, milestone billing)
- ScopeOfWork → BuilderProfile (Work Management → Organization, assignees)

## Token Economics / Revenue Distribution (1 model, requirements stage)

Revenue distribution and token holder economics. New zone introduced by the POWA system.

- [[powa-revenue-distribution model captures epoch-based token revenue sharing]] — requirements stage, epoch-based multi-token revenue distribution with governance lifecycle

Data flow: EpochConfig → RevenueDeposit → DistributionRecord → ClaimEvent

## Local Project Scope

This repository implements 5 of the 21 models (Facet, ResourceTemplate, ServiceOffering, SubscriptionInstance, ResourceInstance) with 4 editors. The POWA revenue distribution model is in requirements stage (not yet implemented). The remaining 16 models exist in the broader Powerhouse ecosystem.

global.description: All 21 Powerhouse domain document models organized by functional area across 5 zones with cross-zone dependencies
